statement.markdown: Write a function to validate the name of a GIT branch. A valid branch name is one that starts with a letter (lowercase or uppercase), followed by any number of alphanumeric characters or hyphens. You can assume that the input will always be a string. The function should return `true` if the branch name is valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the name of the GIT branch
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid
statement.notes: You can use regular expressions to solve this problem.

examples.explanation: The branch name starts with a letter and contains alphanumeric characters and a hyphen
The branch name contains a special character (!)
The branch name starts with a letter and contains alphanumeric characters and hyphens

hints.text: Use a regular expression to match the branch name pattern

editorial.title: Fixing GIT Branch Name Validation
editorial.intuition: The branch name should match a specific pattern
editorial.bruteForce: Try to match each character of the branch name against a set of allowed characters
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match the branch name pattern
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the branch name pattern
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression matches the branch name pattern
commonMistakes: Using a naive approach to check each character
